title: Are we ready for extended life & how to have a sustainable economy - Request

transcript.text: Hello.
I'm going to do a video request from Gypsy Hustled today right now.
So here we are.
So he goes, follow up question to rugged individualism.
According to scientists, it is likely that someday we will be able to reproduce, prevent
Cell degeneration and live forever or use artificial limbs to extend life.
We die not because of the number of years we have lived, but because cell degradation
Increases the odds of a body organ failing.
The problems today, I believe, are caused by myopic planning, short-sighted planning.
The quest for maximum profits within a short time frame is causing devastation to the environment,
And resources are being poorly distributed, leading to exploitations of the needy.
I believe that Marx is correct that capitalism is going to lead towards its own demise and
Result in a revolution.
I believe the revolution will be the result of life extension breakthroughs.
Suddenly people's focus will be on preserving the earth.
Without the earth, we all die desperate life extension technology, everything.
Focus on profits will be replaced with new metrics that are centered on society happiness.
No one would stay at a job they hate forever, because they have infinite time to study and
Do something more rewarding.
Automated machines will also replace the jobs no one wants.
I believe the suffering people endure today – poverty, illness, exploitation, war, terrorism – will
All fade away as we value humanity more.
I think, also, religion will be of less importance in society, as fear of death fades away into the past.
Do you think the human brain is ready for eternal life, and do you think this is the true revolution
We have been waiting for to replace barbaric, exploitative capitalism?
Or do you agree with Richard Dawkins, who says the idea of immortality is a terrifying concept,
Because our life never ends?
Because our life never ends.
Thanks.
